build_reproducibility.details: Single diff: BUILD_DATE in ds-meta changed from 'May 29, 2026' to 'May 30, 2026' (1 line, date-only). This is expected daily behavior — generate-html.js uses new Date() at build time. The published page was last built yesterday. NOT a data-content drift — no data values changed.

issues_found.description: ISS-2216 closed by decider-self-apply-sections.json on 2026-05-29T02:42:43Z with no verification_pattern field — violates PROP-059 discipline. ISS was created and closed within 91 seconds (02:41:12Z → 02:42:43Z). The fix (template grammar for {{CA_DOME}} in Part 2b) does appear to be present in sections.json (problematic patterns not found), but the PROP-059 fingerprint was never written. Per PROP-059 audit rules, this triggers a new ISS.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Verify ISS-2216 target (data/sections.json Part 2b template variables) is correctly fixed; if confirmed, add verification_pattern to the closed entry and tag audit_grandfathered='PROP-059-verified-2026-05-30'. If not confirmed, reopen ISS-2216.

issues_found.description: EXP-tracker next_id=448 but EXP-448 already exists in live items with status='complete'. The next allocation will collide with EXP-448. Likely cause: a writer computed next_id as live_max (448) rather than live_max + 1 (449).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Increment next_id to 449 (or higher if any new items were added since this run). Self-heal: decider should patch next_id = max(live_max, arch_max) + 1.

issues_found.description: 3 live expansion-tracker items are in terminal state and should have been moved to archive: EXP-436 (status=complete, integrated=true), EXP-439 (status=complete, integrated=true), EXP-445 (status=superseded). The live→archive move never fired for these entries.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Move EXP-436, EXP-439, EXP-445 from live items[] to expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl and remove from live. Standard integration writer pattern.

issues_found.description: Expansion-tracker archive has duplicate entries: EXP-394(x2), EXP-395(x2), EXP-396(x2), EXP-397(x2), EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1374(x2), EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1541(x2), EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1590(x2), EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1750(x2), baby-orphan-ISS-1164(x2), baby-orphan-ISS-1781(x2). An atomic write failed to check for existing entries before appending — or two agents appended concurrently.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Deduplicate archive: keep the first occurrence of each id, remove subsequent duplicates. Run dedup script or manual jq filter. Investigate concurrent-write guard in integration writer.

issues_found.description: 12 curmudgeon review files are unprocessed by the decider (latest cycle basis). Breakdown: HOLISTIC c3 reviews x6 (HOL-COMPLETENESS.c3, HOL-CROSSREF.c3, HOL-HIERARCHY.c2, HOL-NARRATIVE.c2, HOL-TAXONOMY.c2, HOL-TONE.c2), WIN cycle upgrades x3 (WIN-004.c7, WIN-029.c5, WIN-064.c6), structural/cross-ref x3 (part1-CROSSREF-c3-verify.c1, part2b-COMPLETENESS, part3-HIERARCHY).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider next run should process these via standard digest intake. The HOLISTIC c3 batch was likely produced by the recent curmudgeon cycle; all are within normal pipeline lag unless any are >48h old.

issues_found.description: docs/index.html BUILD_DATE shows 'May 29, 2026'; rebuild produces 'May 30, 2026'. This is expected — generate-html.js uses new Date() so every build updates the date. No data-content drift. The site was last rebuilt yesterday.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Normal — decider or any agent running build.js will update the date on next publish. No action required.

summary: Overall status FAIL due to one CRITICAL finding (ISS-2216: Mech-A-bypass false closure — decider closed within 91 seconds with no verification_pattern, violating PROP-059 discipline post-cutoff) and four MAJOR findings: (1) EXP-tracker next_id collision imminent (next_id=448=max_id, next allocation will reuse EXP-448); (2) three live tracker items stuck in terminal state needing archive migration; (3) ten duplicate entries in expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl; (4) twelve curmudgeon reviews unprocessed by decider (HOLISTIC c3 batch + WIN cycle upgrades). The site itself is structurally healthy — test suite 3693/3693 passed, all data-source external links OK, discoverability infrastructure intact, no broken review files, no workspace-only data-loss risk, no direction-violation sync issues. The build date diff (May 29→30) is expected daily behavior, not data drift. Priority: decider must fix the next_id collision before the next expansion is allocated; the PROP-059 audit CRITICAL should be verified and grandfathered if ISS-2216 fix is confirmed in sections.json.
